channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: An ex-police officer is convicted of lying about leaks to the Proud Boys leader
item.description: A retired police officer in the nation’s capital has been convicted of lying to authorities about leaking confidential information to the leader of the Proud Boys extremist group.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Chat with Santa or find out if you are on the naughty list with these tech features
item.description: We've moved past simply mailing your wish list to the North Pole. Santa has arrived on ChatGPT and you can talk to him.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KRON4

item.title: House Ethics Committee accuses Gaetz of 'regularly' paying for sex, including with 17-year-old girl
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House Ethics Committee on Monday accused Matt Gaetz of “regularly” paying for sex, including with a 17-year-old girl, and purchasing and using illicit drugs all while the Florida Republican was a member of Congress. Gaetz has denied all wrongdoing.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: New York City police working to ID woman set ablaze in subway and "person of interest" in her death
item.description: New York City police were working Monday to identify the woman who died after being intentionally set on fire inside a stationary subway train as well as more information about a man, now in police custody, who is a “person of interest” in the crime.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden didn't remove these 3 inmates from death row
item.description: Those who received the Monday commutations of their sentences saw them go from execution to life with no possibility of parole. However, three “hard cases” were not among the commutations.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KREX 5

item.title: Missing NC father dead after being shot; daughter found safe
item.description: The father and daughter that have been missing for almost three weeks have been found, according to the Haywood County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Hallie Shuler, NewsNation

item.title: Gaetz Ethics report finds ‘substantial evidence’ of statutory rape, drug use
item.description: The House Ethics Committee found “substantial evidence” that former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) paid a 17-year-old high school student for sex when he was 35, used illicit drugs like cocaine and ecstasy, and obstructed Congress’s investigation into his conduct.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump: Panama will lose control of canal if it continues to 'rip off' US
item.description: President-elect Trump took issue with fees charged to use the Panama Canal, a vital shipping route, on Saturday night, claiming that he will demand oversight of the waterway be given back to the U.S. when he returns to office if it's not changed.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, NewsNation

item.title: Mega Millions jackpot rises to $944 million, to be drawn Tuesday
item.description: Christmas Day could become even more magical for someone if they can match all six winning numbers for a record-setting Mega Millions jackpot.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Luigi Mangione pleads not guilty to state charges in death of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: The man accused of fatally shooting the CEO of UnitedHealthcare pleaded not guilty on Monday to murder and terror charges in a state case that will run parallel to his federal prosecution.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Gaetz sues Ethics Committee in last-minute bid to block release of report
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) on Monday filed a lawsuit against the House Ethics Committee in a last-minute attempt to block the panel from releasing its report on him.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden gives life in prison to 37 of 40 federal death row inmates before Trump can resume executions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden announced on Monday that he is commuting the sentences of 37 of the 40 people on federal death row, converting their punishments to life imprisonment just weeks before President-elect Donald Trump, an outspoken proponent of expanding capital punishment, takes of
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New York City police apprehend suspect in the death of a woman found on fire in a subway car
item.description: New York City police announced Sunday they have in custody a “person of interest” in the early morning death of a woman who they believe may have fallen asleep on a stationary subway train before being intentionally lit on fire by a man she didn't know.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How faith communities can be welcoming of believers with disabilities this holiday season and beyond
item.description: This holiday season, some religious congregations across the U.S. are holding events designed to be accommodating to and inclusive of people with intellectual and developmental disabilities.
item.dc:creator: Mariam Fam And Luis Andres Henao, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden gives life in prison to 37 of 40 federal death row inmates
item.description: The move spares the lives of people convicted in killings, including the slayings of police and military officers, people on federal land and those involved in deadly bank robberies or drug deals, as well as the killings of guards or prisoners in federal facilities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Cowboys beat Tampa Bay 26-24 as Baker Mayfield and the Bucs lose control of their playoff fate
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Baker Mayfield came home to Texas looking to keep his Tampa Bay Buccaneers on track for the playoffs. Instead, they'll need some help.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Abortion opponents shift focus to pills with lawsuits, proposed laws and possible federal action
item.description: Opponents of abortion are increasingly focusing on restricting access to pills, which are the most common way to end a pregnancy in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The new right gathers to celebrate Trump and the splintering of the Republican Party
item.description: That Donald Trump failed to achieve central goals in the government shutdown fight alongside Elon Musk seemed unimportant to many speakers and attendees at Turning Point USA's AmericaFest.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former Israeli spies describe attack using exploding electronic devices against Hezbollah
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Two recently retired senior Israeli intelligence agents shared new details about a deadly clandestine operation years in the making that targeted Hezbollah militants in Lebanon and Syria using exploding pagers and walkie talkies three months ago.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 174 Colorado skiers and snowboarders rescued after a lift cracks
item.description: WINTER PARK, Colo. (AP) — Officials were investigating Sunday what caused a crack in a Colorado ski lift that forced the evacuation of over 170 stranded skiers and snowboarders.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYPD arrests migrant who allegedly set woman on fire on subway train, watched her burn to death
item.description: A migrant from Guatemala allegedly lit a woman on fire on a New York City subway train and stuck around to watch her burn to death just three days before Christmas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Virginia woman faces jail time after practicing illegal dentistry
item.description: A Virginia woman has been convicted of practicing dentistry without a license, following accusations that she illegally performed veneer procedures at her tooth jewelry business.
item.dc:creator: Karina F. Garcia, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump suggests Panama Canal be returned to US control
item.description: President-elect Trump on Sunday again took on the matter of the Panama Canal, suggesting to a conference of his supporters that it be returned to U.S. control in a remarkable suggestion to push a sovereign country to cede territory.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Bucs' Mayfield loses key member of offense before his Texas homecoming
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers have another difficult road test in their attempt to win the NFC South title.
item.dc:creator: Dan Lucas, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Driver killed by police after driving truck through Texas mall, injuring 5
item.description: Authorities said the driver traveled "several hundred yards" through a JCPenney, injuring multiple people.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Jones, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mystery drones could be identified faster using new detection tool, but FAA lacks resources
item.description: The FAA's new "digital license plate" for drones has yet to be rolled out to law enforcement, hindering efforts to identify mystery drones in the Northeast.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amazon workers continue strike four days from Christmas
item.description: Amazon workers at a Staten Island facility are just the latest to walk off the job during the final crucial days of the holiday season.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: In a calendar rarity, Hanukkah starts this year on Christmas Day
item.description: Hanukkah, Judaism’s eight-day Festival of Lights, begins this year on Christmas Day, which has only happened four times since 1900. For some rabbis, the intersection of the two religious holidays provides an auspicious occasion for interfaith engagement.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Here are the 34 Republicans that voted against the funding bill to avoid shutdown
item.description: More than 30 House Republicans voted against the government funding bill to avert a shutdown on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: University of California campuses resolve discrimination complaints stemming from Gaza protests
item.description: The U.S. Department of Education said Friday it has reached an agreement with the University of California system resolving complaints from Jewish and Muslim students of discrimination and harassment during protests last spring over the war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Indiana man sentenced to the maximum of 130 years in prison for 2017 killings of 2 teenage girls
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — An Indiana man convicted in the 2017 killings of two teenage girls who vanished during a winter hike was sentenced to a maximum of 130 years in prison Friday in the case that’s long cast a shadow over the teens’ small hometown of Delphi. Allen, 52, was convicted on Nov.
item.dc:creator: RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 76ers win city council nod to build $1.3B downtown stadium and leave South Philadelphia
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The Philadelphia 76ers won city council approval on Thursday for a $1.3 billion downtown arena despite vocal opposition from nearby Chinatown residents and other activists. Council voted 12-5 to approve the project at a packed meeting at City Hall.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
